titlestmt.titleproper: Burton Bernstein research files on James Thurber8744

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Burton Bernstein research files on James Thurber
did.abstract: Letters, notes, drafts, and source materials compiled or created by Burton Bernstein in the process of the research and writing of his biography of American writer James Thurber, published in 1975. Includes correspondence between Bernstein and E.B. White and Katharine White.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Letters, notes, drafts, and source materials compiled or created by Burton Bernstein in the process of the research and writing of his biography of American writer James Thurber, published in 1975. Includes correspondence between Bernstein and E.B. White and Katharine White.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Burton Bernstein was a staff writer for The New Yorker magazine from 1957 to 1992 and the author of eight books. He is the younger brother of American composer and conductor Leonard Bernstein.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Burton Bernstein research files on James Thurber, #8744. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: William F. Thurber-Interviews Transcripts + Other Papers
"The Middle-Aged Man on the Flying Trapeze"
"The Secret Life of Walter Mitty" (Musical + Opera)
Radio Program on James Thurber Broadcast Over WOSU AM Radio
Dept. of Rare Books - EB White Exhibition 1977
